hypothesis: Incorporating explicit solvent molecule interactions through a hybrid implicit/explicit solvation representation during LoQI training—by augmenting ChEMBL3D conformers with a small number of strategically placed water molecules at hydrogen-bond donor/acceptor sites identified via short molecular dynamics simulations—will yield generated conformers whose solution-phase geometries more accurately predict protein-bound bioactive conformations (as measured by RMSD on the Platinum Diverse dataset) than models trained on implicit-solvent-only geometries, because the explicit solvent shell will encode directional hydrogen-bonding preferences that partially mimic the polar interactions present in protein binding pockets.
